challenges.question: What hidden message is written along the blueprint’s borders?
hints: The message is hidden along the border in faint letters — try reading every third letter.
“Silence” and “Study” seem to be connected — maybe something is beneath it.
'The phrase tells you to explore a location below a room in the mansion.

challenges.question: What single word does this message hint toward?
hints: It’s something you hear — not see.
It repeats after you speak.
The word literally means “the reflection of sound.”

challenges.question: Which combination of True/False answers reveals the correct code to continue?
hints: You must decide which statements about your father’s will are real or lies.
The pattern alternates — not everything false is wrong.
The sequence forms a three-part truth pattern: first and last are false, middle is true.

challenges.question: What area of the mansion does the compass reveal?
hints: Think of direction — north, south, east, or west.
The compass doesn’t point north this time.
The area it indicates is the east section of the mansion.

challenges.question: What word do the runes decode into?
hints: The code uses a Caesar cipher — shift each rune by three.
The word describes what a father leaves behind for his children.
It means “inheritance” or “what you pass on.”

challenges.question: What number sequence opens the next lock?
hints: The numbers have appeared before.
Think back to the tag on the dove’s leg.
The code is those same four digits — nothing more, nothing less.

challenges.question: What object in the mansion does this clue refer to?
hints: Memories are often captured in pictures.
It’s something hanging in plain sight in the main hall.
It shows your entire family together — your father’s favorite item.

challenges.question: What choice opens the final safe — Truth or Lie?
hints: The plaque literally tells you the answer.
Between Truth or Lie, one word opens the way.
Your father always believed truth would prevail.

challenges.question: What is the English translation of “Veritas Vincit”?
hints: Translate the Latin: Veritas = Truth, Vincit = Wins/Prevails.
It’s your father’s motto.
The phrase means “Truth always triumphs.”

challenges.question: What final phrase represents your father’s true lesson and completes the mystery?
hints: Look for a single Latin word engraved on the final key.
It’s the same word your father wrote on many of his letters.
It means “Truth.”
